revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ason]][[ff:Ason]][[fr:Ason]][[ja:朝臣]][[ko:아손]][[uk:Асон (титул)]]
{{Short description|Japanese hereditary noble title|qq=}} {{Other uses|qq=}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019|qq=}} {{Italic title|reason=|qq=}} {{nihongo|'''''Ason'''''|朝臣|Ason, Asomi|qq=}} was a prestigious hereditary [[:en:Japanese nobility|noble]] title in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, used mainly between {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka period}} and {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian periods|1=Heian period}}. At first, it was the second highest, below ''[[Mahito (title)|Mahito]]'', which was given to members of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|lt=Imperial family|1=Imperial House of Japan}}, but after Heian period it became the highest of the eight noble titles:<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|last=Gibney|first=FRank|title=Britannica international encyclopaedia|publisher=TBS-BRITANNICA|year=1995|location=Japan|at=Ason|oclc=55231838|qq=}}</ref> ''Ason, Mahito, [[Sukune]], [[Imiki]], [[Michinoshi]], [[Omi (title)|Omi]], [[Muraji]]'', and ''[[Inagi (title)|Inagi]].''<ref name=":1">{{Cite book|last=Gibney|first=FRank|title=Britannica international encyclopaedia|publisher=TBS-BRITANNICA.|year=1995|location=Japan|at=Yakusanokabane|oclc=55231838|qq=}}</ref> == History == The title was created as a part of the eight-''[[kabane]]'' system, proclaimed in 684 during {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|qid=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}'s reign, as its second highest Rank. As such, ''Asomi'' was initially given to the highest Ranked noble clans whose genealogical origins were often claimed back to imperial princes.<ref name=":1" /> Although the clans closest to the Imperial House, that is, descendants of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}}, were intended to be promoted to ''Mahito'', the first Rank in the original eight ''kabane'' system, this fell out of favor with the nobility. In 802, [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo|Prince Yasuyo]], a son of [[:en:Emperor Kammu|Emperor Kammu]] was designated Yoshimine no Ason Yasuyo, an indication that he had renounced his imperial status and became a member of the peerage. Since the Heian period, ''Ason'' became the highest of the eight ''kabane'', and began to be used by imperial princes and their descendants.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Matsumura|first=Akira|title=Daijisen|publisher=Shōgakkan|year=1995|location=Japan|at=Ason|oclc=949284861|qq=}}</ref> Eventually, all families with aristocratic lineage bore the title of ''Ason'', and the other titles fell out of favor, and ''Ason'' simply became a title indicating a person's social status.<ref name=":0" /> ==Notable holders== Notable ''Ason'' were: * Fujiwara no Ason, awarded by {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} to [[:en:Nakatomi no Kamatari|Nakatomi no Kamatari]] in 668; the origin of the [[Fujiwara clan]] * Minamoto no Ason, first awarded to his non-heir sons by {{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|qid=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}} (786–842); the origin of the [[Minamoto clan]] * Taira no Ason, awarded to his grandson by [[:en:Emperor Kammu|Emperor Kammu]] (737–806); see also [[Taira clan]] Notable holders of ''Ason'' were: * [[:en:Kakinomoto no Ason Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Ason Hitomaro]], poet (c. 662–710) * [[:en:Isonokami no Ason Maro|Isonokami no Ason Maro]], statesman, possibly buried in [[Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] (640–717) * [[:en:Kasa no Ason Maro|Kasa no Ason Maro]], poet better known as Sami Mansei ({{ill|Floruit|en|Floruit|qid=Q36424|lt=fl.|1=Floruit}} 720) * {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|en|Ariwara no Narihira|qid=Q451991|lt=Ariwara no Narihira Ason|1=Ariwara no Narihira}}, poet (825–880) * [[:en:Miyamoto no Kintada Ason|Miyamoto no Kintada Ason]], poet (889–948) * [[:en:Fujiwara Toshiyuki no Ason|Fujiwara no Toshiyuki Ason]], poet (fl. 900) * [[:en:Onakatomi no Yoshinobu Ason|Onakatomi no Yoshinobu Ason]], poet (921–991) * [[:en:Minamoto no Muneyuki Ason|Minamoto no Muneyuki Ason]], poet (d. 983) * [[:en:Fujiwara no Ason Sadaie|Fujiwara no Ason Sadaie]], poet and scholar (1162–1241) * {{ill|Iizasa Ienao|en|Iizasa Ienao|qid=Q861639|lt=Taiganin den Taira no Ason Iga no Kami Raiodo Hon Daikoji|1=Iizasa Ienao}}, founder of [[Tenshin Shōden Katori Shintō-ryū]] (1387–1488) == See also == * ''[[Sukune]]'' * [[Mahito (title)|''Mahito'']] * ''[[Muraji]]'' ==References== {{Reflist|qq=}} {{Japan-hist-stub|qq=}}
[[en:Ason]]
<!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[fr:Ason]] [[ja:朝臣]] [[ko:아손]] [[uk:Асон (титул)]] {{wikidata link|Q4807592}} [[Category:Titles in ancient Japan]] [[Category:政治家・官僚の称号]] [[Category:Samurai]] [[Category:Asuka period]]
